$comment: Sensitive storage ownership policy. Add entries only for committed security-reviewed storage owners.

secretStorageOwners.justification: Encrypted AI provider secrets and local key material are owned only by the shared AI storage seam.
secretStorageOwners.reviewNote: Only encrypted secret envelopes and key material may persist here; plaintext credential fields stay forbidden outside this owner.

secretStorageOwners.justification: The AI provider secret key helper owns transparent local key lookup and in-memory passphrase-derived key resolution.
secretStorageOwners.reviewNote: Transparent local key material may persist only for default obfuscation mode; passphrase-derived key material is in-memory only, and the legacy session key constant exists solely for cleanup and never authorizes persistence.

secretStorageOwners.justification: The AI storage migration seam rewrites legacy plaintext provider records into encrypted secret envelopes.
secretStorageOwners.reviewNote: Migration may touch legacy secret material only to convert it into the canonical encrypted storage format.

secretStorageOwners.justification: The AI passphrase protection seam owns non-secret protection metadata and the in-memory derived-key lifecycle.
secretStorageOwners.reviewNote: Passphrase metadata may persist in local storage, but derived decrypt key material and plaintext passphrases must never persist in local, sync, or session storage; the legacy session key is cleanup-only.

secretStorageOwners.justification: The AI provider secret protection lifecycle seam migrates provider secrets between transparent and passphrase modes.
secretStorageOwners.reviewNote: This owner may rewrite encrypted provider secret envelopes and protection metadata, but it must not persist plaintext passphrases or provider API keys.

sensitiveRetentionOwners.justification: The video preview cache owner persists bounded local-only derived MP4 segments in its dedicated IndexedDB database.
sensitiveRetentionOwners.reviewNote: Only validated AVC preview segments may persist here; records remain advisory, excluded from backup/export/diagnostics, bounded by retention policy, and subject to required erasure verification.

diagnosticSanitizerOwners.justification: Canonical sanitizer owner for persisted or exported diagnostic payloads.
diagnosticSanitizerOwners.reviewNote: New diagnostic collectors should import this seam rather than duplicating sanitization logic.

diagnosticSanitizerOwners.justification: Canonical tracing owner that already centralizes trace payload sanitization for low-level runtime tracing.
diagnosticSanitizerOwners.reviewNote: Tracer internals may stay colocated, but diagnostic collectors outside this seam must import the shared diagnostic sanitizer.

diagnosticSanitizerOwners.justification: Low-level trace console interceptor owned by the canonical tracer seam.
diagnosticSanitizerOwners.reviewNote: Keep console interception and trace sanitization inside the tracer family.

diagnosticSanitizerOwners.justification: Low-level trace event router owned by the canonical tracer seam.
diagnosticSanitizerOwners.reviewNote: New diagnostic persistence/export paths should not move into this file family without an explicit policy update.

diagnosticSanitizerOwners.justification: Trace messaging bridge owned by the canonical tracer seam.
diagnosticSanitizerOwners.reviewNote: Keep tracer transport wiring here; product diagnostics still sanitize through the shared diagnostic seam.

diagnosticSanitizerOwners.justification: Trace transport implementation owned by the canonical tracer seam.
diagnosticSanitizerOwners.reviewNote: Transport internals may remain here, but product diagnostics should not bypass sanitizer ownership.

diagnosticSanitizerOwners.justification: Trace utility helpers owned by the canonical tracer seam.
diagnosticSanitizerOwners.reviewNote: Utility-level sanitization stays internal to tracing; new diagnostic writers should import the canonical sanitizer seam.
